info.description: Filtered OpenAPI description for the stateless synchronous Alibaba Cloud Facebody proxy. The gateway serves only the allowlisted operations defined in the paired YAML manifest.
info.title: Alibaba Cloud Facebody API

post.description: This topic describes the syntax and examples of the BlurFace API for facial information desensitization. The gateway injects Alibaba RPC Action=BlurFace and Version automatically before forwarding the paid request upstream.

ImageURL.description: The image URL. We recommend using an OSS link in the China (Shanghai) region. For files stored locally or via OSS links in other regions, see [File URL Processing](~~155645~~).

Data.description: Result data content.

ImageURL.description:
The URL of the desensitized image.  
> This URL is temporary and has a validity period of 30 minutes. After expiration, the URL will no longer be accessible. If you need to retain the file for an extended period or permanently, access the URL and download the file within 30 minutes, then store it in your own OSS bucket or another storage space.

MaskURL.description:
The mask URL of the desensitized image.  
> This URL is temporary and has a validity period of 30 minutes. After expiration, the URL will no longer be accessible. If you need to retain the file for an extended period or permanently, access the URL and download the file within 30 minutes, then store it in your own OSS bucket or another storage space.

post.description: This topic describes the syntax and sample of the BodyPosture human pose keypoints feature. The gateway injects Alibaba RPC Action=BodyPosture and Version automatically before forwarding the paid request upstream.

ImageURL.description: The URL of the image. We recommend using an OSS link in the China (Shanghai) region. If your file is stored locally or in an OSS bucket outside the China (Shanghai) region, see [File URL Processing](~~155645~~).

Data.description: The returned result data content.

HumanCount.description: The number of detected human bodies.

Label.description: The label of the keypoint. Supported types include: `nose`, `neck`, `right_shoulder`, `right_elbow`, `right_wrist`, `left_shoulder`, `left_elbow`, `left_wrist`, `right_hip`, `right_knee`, `right_ankle`, `left_hip`, `left_knee`, `left_ankle`, `right_eye`, `left_eye`, `right_ear`, `left_ear`.

Positions.description: Keypoint coordinates, provided as relative coordinates with respect to the image dimensions.

items.description:
The [x, y] coordinates of a keypoint.  
- The x coordinate is in the range [0, 1], representing a relative coordinate with respect to the image width. The actual coordinate is x × width.  
- The y coordinate is in the range [0, 1], representing a relative coordinate with respect to the image height. The actual coordinate is y × height.

post.description: This topic describes the syntax and examples of the face comparison (CompareFace) feature. The gateway injects Alibaba RPC Action=CompareFace and Version automatically before forwarding the paid request upstream.

ImageDataA.description:
The Base64-encoded string of Image A. When both URL and Base64 encoding methods are provided, the URL method takes precedence. For instructions on Base64 encoding files, see [File Base64 Processing](~~468191~~).  
> **ImageURLA** and **ImageDataA** are mutually exclusive. When both URL and Base64 encoding methods are provided, the URL method takes precedence.

ImageDataB.description:
The Base64-encoded string of Image B. When both URL and Base64 encoding methods are provided, the URL method takes precedence. For instructions on Base64 encoding files, see [File Base64 Processing](~~468191~~).  
> **ImageURLB** and **ImageDataB** are mutually exclusive. When both URL and Base64 encoding methods are provided, the URL method takes precedence.

ImageURLA.description:
The URL of Image A. We recommend using an OSS link in the Shanghai Region. If your file is stored locally or uses an OSS link outside the Shanghai Region, see [File URL Processing](~~155645~~).  

> **ImageURLA** and **ImageDataA** are mutually exclusive. When both URL and Base64 encoding methods are provided, the URL method takes precedence.

ImageURLB.description:
The URL of Image B. We recommend using an OSS link in the Shanghai Region. If your file is stored locally or uses an OSS link outside the Shanghai Region, see [File URL Processing](~~155645~~).  
> **ImageURLB** and **ImageDataB** are mutually exclusive. When both URL and Base64 encoding methods are provided, the URL method takes precedence.

QualityScoreThreshold.description: The quality score threshold used to evaluate the quality of facial images. If the quality score of a face is below this threshold, a MessageTips field will be added to the return result.

Data.description: The content of the returned result data.

Confidence.description: The confidence level that the largest faces in the two images belong to the same person, with a value range of 0 to 100. Three reference thresholds are 61, 69, and 75, corresponding to false acceptance rates of 1/1,000, 1/10,000, and 1/100,000, respectively. A higher threshold setting results in a lower false acceptance rate and a lower pass rate. For scenarios with higher security requirements, you can set a higher threshold. If no face is detected in either image, an error message is returned.

IsMaskA.description:
Whether the face in Image A is wearing a mask.  
- 0: Not wearing a mask.  
- 1: Wearing a mask.

IsMaskB.description:
Indicates whether the face in Image B is wearing a mask.  
- 0: Not wearing a mask.  
- 1: Wearing a mask.

items.description: The facial landmark coordinates corresponding to the face bounding box in Image A, listed in the following order: horizontal coordinate of the left eye, vertical coordinate of the left eye, horizontal coordinate of the right eye, vertical coordinate of the right eye, horizontal coordinate of the nose tip, vertical coordinate of the nose tip, horizontal coordinate of the left mouth corner, vertical coordinate of the left mouth corner, horizontal coordinate of the right mouth corner, and vertical coordinate of the right mouth corner.

The facial landmark coordinates corresponding to the face bounding box in Image B, listed in the following order: horizontal coordinate of the left eye, vertical coordinate of the left eye, horizontal coordinate of the right eye, vertical coordinate of the right eye, horizontal coordinate of the nose tip, vertical coordinate of the nose tip, horizontal coordinate of the left mouth corner, vertical coordinate of the left mouth corner, horizontal coordinate of the right mouth corner, and vertical coordinate of the right mouth corner.

MessageTips.description:
Prompt messages in plain text. The following prompts may appear individually or as a combination:  

Quality-related prompts (appear when the quality score of a face is lower than the quality score threshold specified in the request parameters):  
- `imageA quality score less threshold`: The quality score of Image A is below the threshold set in the input parameters.  
- `imageB quality score less threshold`: The quality score of Image B is below the threshold set in the input parameters.  

Face size-related prompts (appear when a face is detected and either its width or height is less than 50 pixels. When this prompt appears, you should provide faces with resolution greater than 64×64 pixels where possible, and consider the returned Confidence value accordingly):  
- `face in imageA is too small`: The face in Image A is too small.  
- `face in imageB is too small`: The face in Image B is too small.

QualityScoreA.description: Quality score of input Image A.

QualityScoreB.description: Quality score of input Image B.

items.description: The bounding box `[left, top, width, height]` of the largest face in Image A, for example, `[157,82,147,188]`.
The bounding box `[left, top, width, height]` of the largest face in Image B, for example, `[169,48,125,162]`.

Confidence thresholds corresponding to false acceptance rates (FAR) of 1/1000, 1/10000, and 1/100000, respectively. For example, `[61.0, 69.0, 75.0]` indicates that at an FAR of 1/1000, a Confidence value exceeding 61% suggests the two faces belong to the same person.

post.description: This topic describes the syntax and examples of the masked face comparison 1:1 (CompareFaceWithMask) feature. The gateway injects Alibaba RPC Action=CompareFaceWithMask and Version automatically before forwarding the paid request upstream.

ImageURLA.description: The URL of Image A. We recommend that you use an OSS link in the China (Shanghai) region. If your file is stored locally or uses an OSS link in a region other than China (Shanghai), see [File URL Processing](~~155645~~).

ImageURLB.description: The URL of Image B. We recommend that you use an OSS link in the China (Shanghai) region. If your file is stored locally or uses an OSS link in a region other than China (Shanghai), see [File URL Processing](~~155645~~).

QualityScoreThreshold.description: Quality score threshold used to analyze and evaluate the quality of facial images. If the quality score of a face is lower than this threshold, a MessageTips field will be added to the Return Result to provide a corresponding explanation.

Data.description: The content of the returned result data.

Confidence.description: The confidence level that the largest faces in the two images belong to the same person, with a value range of 0 to 100. Three reference thresholds are 61, 69, and 75, corresponding to false acceptance rates of 1/1,000, 1/10,000, and 1/100,000, respectively. A higher threshold setting results in a lower false acceptance rate and a lower pass rate. For scenarios with higher security requirements, you can set a higher threshold. If no face is detected in either image, an error message is returned.

IsMaskA.description:
Indicates whether the face in image A is wearing a mask.  

- 0: Not wearing a mask  
- 1: Wearing a mask

IsMaskB.description:
Indicates whether the face in image B is wearing a mask.  

- 0: Not wearing a mask  
- 1: Wearing a mask

LandmarksAList.description: An array of facial landmark coordinates corresponding to the face bounding box in Image A.

items.description: Facial landmark coordinates corresponding to the face bounding box in Image A, in the following order: horizontal coordinate of the left eye, vertical coordinate of the left eye, horizontal coordinate of the right eye, vertical coordinate of the right eye, horizontal coordinate of the nose tip, vertical coordinate of the nose tip, horizontal coordinate of the left mouth corner, vertical coordinate of the left mouth corner, horizontal coordinate of the right mouth corner, and vertical coordinate of the right mouth corner.

LandmarksBList.description: An array of facial landmark coordinates corresponding to the face bounding box in Image B.

items.description: Facial landmark coordinates corresponding to the face bounding box in Image B, in the following order: horizontal coordinate of the left eye, vertical coordinate of the left eye, horizontal coordinate of the right eye, vertical coordinate of the right eye, horizontal coordinate of the nose tip, vertical coordinate of the nose tip, horizontal coordinate of the left mouth corner, vertical coordinate of the left mouth corner, horizontal coordinate of the right mouth corner, and vertical coordinate of the right mouth corner.

MessageTips.description:
Tip messages, provided as plain text descriptions. These messages may appear individually or in combination.  

Quality-related tips (appear when the quality score of a face is below the quality score threshold specified in the request parameters):  

- `imageA quality score less threshold`: The quality score of image A is below the threshold set in the input parameters.  
- `imageB quality score less threshold`: The quality score of image B is below the threshold set in the input parameters.  

Face size-related tips (appear when a face is detected and its width or height is less than 50 pixels. When this message appears, you should provide faces with pixel dimensions greater than 64×64 whenever possible, and consider the returned confidence level accordingly):  

- `face in imageA is too small`: The face in image A is too small.  
- `face in imageB is too small`: The face in image B is too small.

QualityScoreA.description: The quality score of input image A.

QualityScoreB.description: The quality score of input image B.

RectAList.description: The bounding rectangle of the largest face in Image A.

items.description: The bounding rectangle of the largest face in Image A as [left, top, width, height], for example, [238, 310, 388, 492].

RectBList.description: The bounding rectangle of the largest face in Image B.

items.description: The bounding rectangle of the largest face in Image B as [left, top, width, height], for example, [284, 467, 293, 373].

Thresholds.description: An array of confidence thresholds corresponding to different false acceptance rates.

items.description: Confidence thresholds corresponding to false acceptance rates of 1/1000, 1/10000, and 1/100000, respectively. For example, `[61.0, 69.0, 75.0]` indicates that, at a false acceptance rate of 1/1000, a confidence level exceeding 61% suggests that the two faces belong to the same person.

post.description: This topic describes the syntax and examples of DeepfakeFace under the facebody category. The gateway injects Alibaba RPC Action=DeepfakeFace and Version automatically before forwarding the paid request upstream.

ImageData.description:
Base64-encoded string of the image. When both Base64 and URL are provided, the URL takes precedence.  
For instructions on Base64 encoding files, see [File Base64 Processing](~~468191~~).

ImageURL.description:
The URL of the image to be detected. Each element in the JSON array is an image detection task structure (image Table). Up to 10 elements are supported, meaning you can detect up to 10 images simultaneously. We recommend using an OSS link in the China (Shanghai) region. For local files or OSS links in other regions, see [File URL Processing](~~155645~~).

> For this API, the system automatically converts the OSS URL to an internal network address before downloading the file. Therefore, invoking this API does not incur OSS traffic billing.

Data.description: The content of the returned result data.

FaceNumber.description:
The number of faces detected in a single image.  
If no face is detected, FaceNumber returns 0.

ImageURL.description: The image URL address corresponding to the request.

Results.description: The return result. When the invocation succeeds, the result contains one or more elements.

Confidence.description: The value range is [0.00, 100.00]. A lower value indicates a higher probability that the face is genuine.

Label.description:
The categorization of the detection result. Valid values include:

- normalface: genuine face image
- deepfakeface: face-swapped image

If no face is detected, the Label returns `deepfake`.

MessageTips.description:
Prompt messages.  
- If the input face is blurred, occluded, or at a large angle, the following message is returned: `blurred, occluded or large angle face, please check.`  
- If no face is detected, the following message is returned: `no face detected, try again please.`
MessageTips.example: blurred, occluded or large angle face, please check.

Height.description: The height of the detection bounding box.

Left.description: The x coordinate of the top-left point of the detection box.

Top.description: The y-coordinate of the top-left point of the detection bounding box.

Width.description: The width of the detection bounding box.

TaskId.description: The ID of the detection job.

post.description: This topic describes the syntax and examples of the DetectBodyCount API. The gateway injects Alibaba RPC Action=DetectBodyCount and Version automatically before forwarding the paid request upstream.

ImageURL.description: The image URL. We recommend using an OSS link in the China (Shanghai) region. For files stored locally or via an OSS link in a different region, see [File URL Processing](~~155645~~).

Data.description: The returned result data content.

post.description: This topic describes the syntax and examples of the DetectCelebrity API. The gateway injects Alibaba RPC Action=DetectCelebrity and Version automatically before forwarding the paid request upstream.

ImageURL.description: The URL of the image. We recommend using an OSS link in the China (Shanghai) region. For images stored locally or via an OSS link in a different region, see [File URL Processing](~~155645~~).

Data.description: The content of the returned result data.

items.description: Coordinate information of the face bounding box. The format is `[x, y, width, height]`. x and y represent the coordinates of the top-left corner of the face box, while width and height represent the width and height of the face box. The values of x, y, width, and height are all normalized ratios relative to the width and height of the input image.

post.description: This topic describes the syntax and examples of the DetectFace operation for face detection and facial landmark localization. The gateway injects Alibaba RPC Action=DetectFace and Version automatically before forwarding the paid request upstream.

ImageURL.description: The image URL. We recommend that you use an OSS link in the China (Shanghai) region. If your file is stored locally or in an OSS bucket in a different region, see [File URL Processing](~~155645~~).

Landmark.description: Specifies whether to return facial feature point localization. Valid values: true or false.

MaxFaceNumber.description: Sets the maximum number of faces to return in the image. Valid values: 1 to 30. To return detection results for multiple faces, set this parameter correctly. By default, results are sorted in descending order based on the FaceProbabilityList parameter.

Pose.description: Specifies whether to return face pose information. Valid values: true or false.

Quality.description: Specifies whether to return face quality information. Valid values: true or false.

Data.description: The returned result data content.

items.description: The probability that the screenshot contains a face, with values ranging from 0 to 1. If multiple faces are present, probabilities are returned in sequence. For example, if two faces exist, the response is `[face_prob1, face_prob2]`. A higher value indicates a larger facial area resolution, clearer facial details, and a more frontal view of the face.

Returns face bounding rectangles as `[left, top, width, height]`. If multiple faces are detected, rectangles are appended sequentially. For example, two faces yield `[left1, top1, width1, height1, left2, top2, width2, height2]`.

left-top: The top-left corner of the bounding box, using the image’s top-left corner as the coordinate origin (x, y). This point is x pixels from the left border and y pixels from the top border of the image.  
width-height: The width and height of the bounding box.  
The bounding box area equals width × height, and its bottom-right coordinate is (left + width, top + height).

LandmarkCount.description: Number of facial feature points, currently fixed at 105 points. These include: 24 points for eyebrows, 32 for eyes, 6 for nose, 34 for mouth, and 9 for the outer contour.

items.description: Comprehensive score for keypoint detection, with values in the range (0, 100]. A threshold of 85 is recommended (you can determine whether to apply a threshold and its value based on your actual application scenario). A higher score indicates better quality of facial keypoints, which improves detection performance and can be used to filter face samples.

The facial feature point localization result. Each face returns a group of feature point coordinates in the format `(x0, y0, x1, y1, ...)`. If multiple faces are present, their coordinates are appended sequentially as floating-point numbers.

Returns the face pose angles in the format `[yaw, pitch, roll]`. If multiple faces are detected, the angles are listed in order.

- Yaw represents the left-right angle, with a range of -90 to 90 degrees.  
- Pitch represents the up-down angle, with a range of -90 to 90 degrees.  
- Roll represents the in-plane rotation angle, with a range of -180 to 180 degrees.

The centroid coordinates and radius of the left and right pupils. Each face is represented by six floating-point numbers in the order: `[left_iris_cenpt.x, left_iris_cenpt.y, left_iris_radius, right_iris_cenpt.x, right_iris_cenpt.y, right_iris_radis]`.

Qualities.description: The quality assessment of detected faces. A higher score indicates better suitability for detection.

items.description: The impact score of facial blur on detection. A higher score indicates better suitability for detection, with a value range of (0, 100]. If multiple faces are present, scores are returned sequentially. During detection, it is recommended to set a threshold of at least 85 (you can determine whether to set a threshold and its value based on your actual application scenario). A score above 85 indicates a lower probability of image blur, while a score below 85 indicates a higher probability of image blur.

The score indicating whether the target is a face and its impact on detection. A higher score is more favorable for detection, with a value range of (0, 100]. If multiple faces are present, the scores are listed sequentially. During detection, it is recommended to set a threshold greater than or equal to 85 (you can decide whether to set a threshold and its value based on your actual application scenario). A score above 85 indicates a higher probability that the image contains a face, while a score below 85 indicates a lower probability.

The score indicating the impact of upper-face occlusions, such as glasses, on detection. A higher score is more favorable for detection, with a value range of (0, 100]. If multiple faces are present, the scores are listed sequentially. During detection, it is recommended to set a threshold greater than or equal to 85 (you can decide whether to set a threshold and its value based on your actual application scenario). A score above 85 indicates a lower probability of wearing glasses, while a score below 85 indicates a higher probability of wearing glasses.

The score indicating the impact of illumination on detection. A higher score is more favorable for detection, with a value range of (0, 100]. If multiple faces are present, the scores are listed sequentially. During detection, it is recommended to set a threshold greater than or equal to 85 (you can decide whether to set a threshold and its value based on your actual application scenario). A score above 85 indicates a higher probability of good lighting in the image, while a score below 85 indicates a lower probability of good lighting.

The score indicating the impact of lower-face occlusions, such as masks, on detection. A higher score is more favorable for detection, with a value range of (0, 100]. If multiple faces are present, the scores are listed sequentially. During detection, it is recommended to set a threshold greater than or equal to 85 (you can decide whether to set a threshold and its value based on your actual application scenario). A score above 85 indicates a lower probability of wearing a mask, while a score below 85 indicates a higher probability of wearing a mask.

The impact score of image noise on detection. A higher score indicates better suitability for detection, with a value range of (0, 100]. If multiple faces are present, scores are returned sequentially. During detection, it is recommended to set a threshold of at least 85 (you can determine whether to set a threshold and its value based on your actual application scenario). A score above 85 indicates a lower probability of image noise, while a score below 85 indicates a higher probability of image noise.

The impact score of pose on detection. A higher score indicates better conditions for detection, with a value range of (0, 100]. If multiple faces are detected, the scores are listed in order. During detection, it is recommended to set a threshold greater than or equal to 85 (you can determine whether to set a threshold and its value based on your actual application scenario). A score above 85 indicates a higher probability that the face is frontal, while a score below 85 indicates a lower probability.

The overall quality score. A higher score indicates better suitability for detection, with a value range of (0, 100]. If multiple faces are present, scores are returned sequentially. During detection, it is recommended to set a threshold of at least 85 (you can determine whether to set a threshold and its value based on your actual application scenario). A score above 85 indicates higher overall image quality, while a score below 85 indicates lower overall image quality.

post.description: This topic describes the syntax and examples of the infrared face liveness detection operation DetectInfraredLivingFace. The gateway injects Alibaba RPC Action=DetectInfraredLivingFace and Version automatically before forwarding the paid request upstream.

Tasks.description: An array of image URLs to be detected.

ImageURL.description:
The URL of the image to be detected. Each element in the JSON array is an image detection task struct (image table). A maximum of 10 elements is supported, meaning up to 10 images can be processed simultaneously. It is recommended to use OSS links from the China (Shanghai) region. For files stored locally or via OSS links in other regions, see [File URL Processing](~~155645~~).

> For this API, the system automatically converts the OSS URL to an internal network address before downloading the file. Therefore, invoking this API does not incur OSS traffic billing.

Data.description: The returned result data content.

FaceNumber.description: The number of faces detected in a single image. If no face is detected, FaceNumber returns 0.

ImageURL.description: The image URL from the corresponding request.

Results.description: Returns facial coordinates and liveness detection results.

Label.description:
The class of the detection result. Valid values include:  
- normal: live photo  
- liveness: replayed photo  

If no face is detected, Label is returned as liveness.

MessageTips.description:
Prompt message, in plain text description.

- `blurred, occluded or large angle face, please check.`: Indicates that the input face is blurred, occluded, or captured at a large angle.
- `no face detected, try again please.`: Indicates that no face was detected.
MessageTips.example: blurred, occluded or large angle face, please check.

Rate.description:
The probability (in percent) that the result belongs to this class, with a value range of [0.00–100.00]. A higher value indicates a higher probability of belonging to this class.  
If no face is detected, Rate is returned as 100.

Rect.description: Face bounding box information. If no face is detected, all coordinates in Rect are returned as 0.

Height.description: The height of the detection bounding box.

Left.description: The x-coordinate of the top-left point of the detection bounding box.

Top.description: The y-coordinate of the top-left point of the detection bounding box.

Width.description: The width of the detection bounding box.

Suggestion.description:
The recommended operation for you to perform. Valid values are as follows:

- pass: The liveness object in the image comes from direct capture. No further action is required.
- review: The liveness object in the image might be an attack sample. You should confirm before proceeding.
- block: The liveness object in the image is highly likely to be an attack sample. You should perform the subsequent operation.

If no face is detected, the Suggestion field returns block.

post.description: This topic describes the syntax and examples of the face liveness detection (DetectLivingFace) feature. The gateway injects Alibaba RPC Action=DetectLivingFace and Version automatically before forwarding the paid request upstream.

ImageData.description:
Base64-encoded string of the image. If both ImageData and ImageURL are provided, ImageURL takes precedence.  
For instructions on Base64 encoding files, see [File Base64 Processing](~~468191~~).

ImageURL.description:
URL of the image to be detected. Each element in the JSON array represents an image detection task (image structure). Up to 10 elements are supported, meaning up to 10 images can be processed simultaneously. It is recommended to use OSS links in the China (Shanghai) region. For local files or OSS links outside the China (Shanghai) region, see [File URL Processing](~~155645~~).  

> For this API, the system automatically converts the OSS URL to an internal network address before downloading the file. Therefore, invoking this API does not incur OSS traffic billing.

Data.description: The content of the returned result data.

FaceNumber.description:
Number of faces detected in a single image.  
If no face is detected, FaceNumber returns 0.

ImageURL.description: Image URL address corresponding to the request.

Results.description: The return results. When the invocation succeeds, the results contain one or more elements.

Frames.description: **(This field is deprecated.)** When the input image is a long image (with an aspect ratio greater than 3:1), the original image is cropped into several images with normal aspect ratios. The frames field stores the cropped screenshots that were hit as screen replays.

Rate.description:
The value indicates the probability of the result belonging to this category, ranging from [0.00, 100.00]. A higher value indicates a greater probability of belonging to this category.  
If no face is detected, Rate is returned as 100.

Url.description: The screenshot where a hit occurred.

Label.description:
Category of the detection result. Valid values include:  
- normal: live face image  
- liveness: replayed or spoofed image  

If no face is detected, the Label returns "liveness".

MessageTips.description:
The tip message.  
- If the input face is blurred, occluded, or captured at a large angle, the following message is returned: `blurred, occluded or large angle face, please check.`  
- If no face is detected, the following message is returned: `no face detected, try again please.`
MessageTips.example: blurred, occluded or large angle face, please check.

Rate.description: Probability of the result belonging to this category, with a value range of [0.00, 100.00]. A higher value indicates a greater probability of belonging to this category.

Height.description: The height of the detection bounding box.

Left.description: The x-coordinate of the top-left point of the detection bounding box.

Top.description: The y-coordinate of the top-left point of the detection bounding box.

Width.description: Width of the detection bounding box.

Suggestion.description:
The recommended operation for you to perform. Valid values are as follows:  
- pass: The liveness object in the image comes from direct capture. No further action is required.  
- review: The liveness object in the image might come from screen replay. We recommend that you confirm before proceeding.  
- block: The liveness object in the image most likely comes from screen replay. We recommend that you take the subsequent actions.  

If no face is detected, Suggestion returns block.

TaskId.description: The ID of the detection job.

post.description: This topic describes the syntax and examples of the DetectPedestrian human detection feature. The gateway injects Alibaba RPC Action=DetectPedestrian and Version automatically before forwarding the paid request upstream.

ImageURL.description:
The image URL. We recommend using an OSS link in the China (Shanghai) region. If your file is stored locally or in an OSS bucket in a different region, see [File URL Processing](~~155645~~).

Input constraints:

- Image format: JPEG, JPG, PNG, or BMP.
- Image size: No larger than 4 MB.
- Image resolution: Resolution greater than 32×32 and less than 2000×2000 pixels, with the longest edge shorter than 2000 pixels. The detected human face must occupy at least 64×64 pixels.
- The URL must not contain Chinese characters.

> If the image resolution exceeds the maximum limit, scale down the image first. For details, see [Image Scaling](~~44688~~).

Data.description: Returned data content.

Elements.description: Collection of human detection bounding boxes.

items.description: Bounding box coordinates in the format `[left, top, right, bottom]`. Here, `left` is the x-coordinate of the top-left corner of the human bounding box, `top` is the y-coordinate of the top-left corner, `right` is the x-coordinate of the bottom-right corner, and `bottom` is the y-coordinate of the bottom-right corner.

Score.description: Confidence level of the bounding box, with a value range from 0 to 1. A score closer to 1 indicates a higher probability that the human bounding box is accurate.

Type.description: Type of the detection result corresponding to the bounding box. Only the parameter value `person` is returned.

Height.description: Height of the input image, in pixels.

Width.description: Width of the input image, in pixels.

post.description: This topic describes the syntax and examples of the DetectVideoLivingFace API for video liveness detection. The gateway injects Alibaba RPC Action=DetectVideoLivingFace and Version automatically before forwarding the paid request upstream.

VideoUrl.description: The URL of the video containing the face to be detected. We recommend using an OSS link in the China (Shanghai) region. For files stored locally or in an OSS bucket outside the China (Shanghai) region, see [File URL Processing](~~155645~~).

Data.description: The content of the returned result data.

FaceConfidence.description: Confidence level of the detected face, with a value range of 0 to 1.

LiveConfidence.description: Confidence level of liveness detection, with a value range of 0 to 1.

items.description: Position of the detected face.

post.description: This topic describes the syntax and examples of the EnhanceFace feature for face restoration and enhancement. The gateway injects Alibaba RPC Action=EnhanceFace and Version automatically before forwarding the paid request upstream.

ImageURL.description:
The image URL. We recommend using an OSS link from the China (Shanghai) region. For local files or OSS links from regions other than Shanghai, see [File URL Processing](~~155645~~).  

Input constraints:  

- Image format: JPEG, JPG, BMP, PNG.  
- File size: No larger than 3 MB.  
- Image resolution: Greater than 32×32 pixels and less than 2048×2048 pixels (the longest edge must be no more than 2047 pixels), with the face occupying at least 64×64 pixels.  
- The URL must not contain Chinese characters.  
- The input image must contain at least one face.  
- The number of faces in the input image should not exceed 10; otherwise, only the 10 largest faces by area will be processed.  
- The faces in the input image should not have scratches, damage, or similar issues, as the algorithm does not currently support repairing such defects.  
- The face quality in the input image should not be excessively high or the resolution too large, as this may lead to unintended quality degradation.  

> If the image resolution exceeds the maximum limit, scale down the image first. For details, see [Image Scaling](~~44688~~).

Data.description: The content of the returned result data.

ImageURL.description:
The URL of the enhanced face restoration result image.  
> This URL is temporary and has a validity period of 30 minutes. After expiration, the URL will no longer be accessible. To retain the file permanently or for an extended duration, you must access the URL and download the file within 30 minutes, then store it in your own OSS bucket or other storage space.

post.description: This topic describes the syntax and sample of the ExtractFingerprint operation. The gateway injects Alibaba RPC Action=ExtractFingerPrint and Version automatically before forwarding the paid request upstream.

ImageData.description:
The Base64-encoded string of the image. For instructions on Base64 encoding of files, see [File Base64 Processing](~~468191~~).

> You must specify either **ImageURL** or **ImageData**. If both the URL and Base64 encoding methods are provided, the URL method takes precedence.

ImageURL.description:
The image URL. We recommend using an OSS link in the China (Shanghai) region. If your file is stored locally or in an OSS bucket outside the China (Shanghai) region, see [File URL Processing](~~155645~~).

> You must specify either **ImageURL** or **ImageData**. If both the URL and Base64 encoding methods are provided, the URL method takes precedence. For instructions on Base64 encoding of files, see [File Base64 Processing](~~468191~~).

Data.description: The content of the returned result data.

FingerPrint.description: The Base64-encoded string of the fingerprint extraction result image.

post.description: This topic describes the syntax and sample of the FaceBeauty retouching feature. The gateway injects Alibaba RPC Action=FaceBeauty and Version automatically before forwarding the paid request upstream.

ImageURL.description:
The image URL. We recommend using an OSS link in the China (Shanghai) region. For images stored locally or via OSS links in other regions, see [File URL Processing](~~155645~~).  
Input restrictions:  

- Image format: JPEG, JPG, PNG, BMP.  
- File size: No larger than 4 MB.  
- Image resolution: Must be greater than 10×10 pixels and less than 2000×2000 pixels, with the longest edge under 2000 pixels.  
- The URL must not contain Chinese characters.  
- Recommended image quality: Suitable for portrait images of most skin types. Results may be suboptimal for images with severe freckles, acne, underexposure, or similar conditions.

Sharp.description: Sharpening (definition) degree, with a value range of 0 to 1. A value closer to 1 indicates a higher sharpening effect.

Smooth.description: Smoothing degree, with a value range of 0 to 1. A value closer to 1 indicates a higher smoothing effect.

White.description: Whitening degree, with a value range of 0 to 1. A value closer to 1 indicates a higher whitening effect.

Data.description: The returned data content.

ImageURL.description:
The URL of the retouched image.  
> This URL is temporary and has a validity period of 30 minutes. After expiration, the URL will no longer be accessible. To retain the file permanently or for an extended duration, you must access the URL and download the file within 30 minutes, then store it in your own OSS bucket or other storage space.

post.description: This topic describes the syntax and examples of the GenerateHumanAnimeStyle operation. The gateway injects Alibaba RPC Action=GenerateHumanAnimeStyle and Version automatically before forwarding the paid request upstream.

parameters.description:
Image URL. We recommend using an OSS link in the China (Shanghai) region. If your file is stored locally or in an OSS bucket outside the China (Shanghai) region, see [File URL Processing](~~155645~~).  

Image format requirements:  

- Image formats: JPG, JPEG, PNG.  
- File size: No larger than 3 MB.  
- Image resolution: Greater than 32×32 pixels and less than 2000×2000 pixels. The longest edge must be no more than 1999 pixels, and the face area must be at least 60×60 pixels.  
- Supports clear frontal photos containing one or multiple faces, with a maximum of five faces. Face orientation must be within 45 degrees in any direction (up, down, left, or right).  
- The URL must not contain Chinese characters.  

> Image content: If the image resolution exceeds the maximum limit, scale down the image first. For details, see [Image Scaling](~~44688~~).
parameters.name: ImageURL

schema.description:
Image URL. We recommend using an OSS link in the China (Shanghai) region. If your file is stored locally or in an OSS bucket outside the China (Shanghai) region, see [File URL Processing](~~155645~~).  

Image format requirements:  

- Image formats: JPG, JPEG, PNG.  
- File size: No larger than 3 MB.  
- Image resolution: Greater than 32×32 pixels and less than 2000×2000 pixels. The longest edge must be no more than 1999 pixels, and the face area must be at least 60×60 pixels.  
- Supports clear frontal photos containing one or multiple faces, with a maximum of five faces. Face orientation must be within 45 degrees in any direction (up, down, left, or right).  
- The URL must not contain Chinese characters.  

> Image content: If the image resolution exceeds the maximum limit, scale down the image first. For details, see [Image Scaling](~~44688~~).

parameters.description:
Cartoon effect configuration parameter. Valid values:  

- anime: Japanese anime style  
- 3d: 3D effect  
- handdrawn: Hand-drawn style  
- sketch: Pencil sketch  
- artstyle: Artistic effect  
- claborate: Traditional Chinese fine-brush painting  
- hongkong: Hong Kong comic style  
- comic: Comic style  
- animation3d: Animated 3D style
parameters.name: AlgoType

schema.description:
Cartoon effect configuration parameter. Valid values:  

- anime: Japanese anime style  
- 3d: 3D effect  
- handdrawn: Hand-drawn style  
- sketch: Pencil sketch  
- artstyle: Artistic effect  
- claborate: Traditional Chinese fine-brush painting  
- hongkong: Hong Kong comic style  
- comic: Comic style  
- animation3d: Animated 3D style

Data.description: The returned result data content.

ImageURL.description:
The URL of the cartoonized image result.  
> This URL is temporary and valid for 30 minutes. After expiration, the URL will no longer be accessible. To store the file permanently or for an extended period, you must access the URL and download the file within 30 minutes, then save it to your own OSS bucket or other storage space.

post.description: Add human portrait sketch stylization. The gateway injects Alibaba RPC Action=GenerateHumanSketchStyle and Version automatically before forwarding the paid request upstream.

ImageURL.description:
The image URL. We recommend using an OSS link in the China (Shanghai) region. If your file is stored locally or in an OSS bucket outside the China (Shanghai) region, see [File URL Processing](~~155645~~).  

Image format requirements:  

- Image formats: JPG, JPEG, PNG.  
- File size: less than 10 MB.  
- Image resolution: greater than 100×100 pixels and less than 2000×2000 pixels, with the longest edge no more than 1999 pixels; the face must occupy at least 60×60 pixels.  
- Image quality: clear frontal photos containing a single person’s face, with the face oriented within 45 degrees up, down, left, or right.  
- The URL must not contain Chinese characters.  

> Image content: If the image resolution exceeds the maximum limit, scale down the image first. For details, see [Image Scaling](~~44688~~).

ReturnType.description:
The form in which the image is returned. Valid values:  

- head: headshot (default)  
- full: full-body photo  

We recommend using headshots for better processing results.

Data.description: The content of the returned result data.

ImageURL.description:
The URL of the returned image.  
> This URL is temporary and has a validity period of 30 minutes. It will expire and become inaccessible after this period.

post.description: This topic describes the syntax and examples of the Intelligent LiquifyFace feature. The gateway injects Alibaba RPC Action=LiquifyFace and Version automatically before forwarding the paid request upstream.

ImageURL.description:
Image URL. It is recommended to use an OSS link in the China (Shanghai) region. For local files or OSS links in other regions, see [File URL Processing](~~155645~~).  
Image format requirements:  

- Image formats: JPG, JPEG, PNG.  
- File size: No larger than 6 MB.  
- Image resolution: At least 128×128 pixels and at most 5000×5000 pixels, with the longest edge no greater than 5000 pixels.  
- Image content: Must contain at least one face and no more than three faces, with each face occupying an area of at least 64×64 pixels.  
- The URL must not contain Chinese characters.  
> Image content: If the image resolution exceeds the maximum limit, scale down the image first. For details, see [Image Scaling](~~44688~~).

SlimDegree.description: Standard intensity. The value range is [0, 2.0], with a default value of 1.0. A higher value results in a more pronounced face slimming effect.

Data.description: Returned data content.

ImageURL.description:
URL of the image after face slimming.  
> This URL is temporary and has a validity period of 30 minutes. After expiration, it will no longer be accessible. If you need to store the file for a longer duration or permanently, access the URL and download the file within 30 minutes, then save it to your own OSS bucket or other storage space.

post.description: This topic describes the syntax and examples of the MergeImageFace operation. The gateway injects Alibaba RPC Action=MergeImageFace and Version automatically before forwarding the paid request upstream.

AddWatermark.description:
Specifies whether to add a watermark. Valid values:  

- False (default): Do not add a watermark.  
- True: Add a watermark.

ImageURL.description:
The facial image to be merged. This parameter is used only when the template image contains a single person. We recommend using an OSS link from the China (Shanghai) region. If your file is stored locally or in an OSS bucket outside the China (Shanghai) region, see [File URL Processing](~~155645~~).  
Input constraints:  
- Image format: JPG, JPEG, or PNG.  
- File size: No larger than 20 MB.  
- Image resolution: At least 128×128 pixels and at most 2000×2000 pixels, with the longest edge no greater than 2000 pixels.  
- The URL must not contain Chinese characters.  

> - If the image resolution exceeds the maximum limit, scale down the image first. For more information, see [Image Scaling](~~44688~~).  
- You must specify either **ImageURL** or **MergeInfos**, but not both.

items.description:
Used only when the template image contains multiple people. Specifies the URL of the replacement image for each face in the template.  

> You must specify either **ImageURL** or **MergeInfos**, but not both.

ImageURL.description: The URL of the image to replace this face.

TemplateFaceID.description: The sub-ID of a specific face in a multi-face template.

ModelVersion.description: Two model versions are available: v1 supports face-shape adaptation, while v2 does not. These versions produce visibly different results. The default value is v1 (the face-shape adaptation version).

TemplateId.description:
Template ID of the face template.  

> You must first upload the template. For details, see [Increase Image Face Fusion Template](~~203512~~).

WatermarkType.description:
The watermark type. Valid values:  

- EN (default): English watermark.  
- CN: Chinese watermark.

Data.description: The content of the returned result data.

ImageURL.description:
The URL of the merged image.  
> This URL is temporary and valid for 30 minutes. After expiration, the URL will no longer be accessible. To store the file permanently or for an extended period, you must access the URL and download the file within 30 minutes, then save it to your own OSS bucket or other storage space.

post.description: This topic describes the syntax and examples of the online proctoring (MonitorExamination) feature. The gateway injects Alibaba RPC Action=MonitorExamination and Version automatically before forwarding the paid request upstream.

ImageURL.description: Image URL. We recommend using an OSS link in the China (Shanghai) region. For files stored locally or via an OSS link in a different region, see [File URL Processing](~~155645~~).

Type.description:
Detection type.  

- 0: Screen chat tool detection  
- 1: Candidate status detection

Data.description: The content of the returned result data.

ChatScore.description:
The probability that the screen capture contains a chat application, with a value range of (0,1).
This parameter is displayed when the request parameter Type is set to 0.

FaceInfo.description: Face information of the central person. This parameter is displayed when the request parameter Type is set to 1.

Completeness.description: Face integrity score, with a value range of (0,1).

FaceNumber.description: Number of detected faces. A value of 0 indicates that no examinee's face was detected.

Pose.description: When facing the camera directly, all three angles are 0. The value range is between −90 and +90 degrees.

Pitch.description: Pitch angle: positive values indicate tilting the head upward, and negative values indicate tilting the head downward.

Roll.description: Roll angle: positive values indicate clockwise rotation of the face in the image, and negative values indicate counterclockwise rotation.

Yaw.description: Yaw angle: positive values indicate looking to the left, and negative values indicate looking to the right.

PersonInfo.description: The property and behavior information of the central person. This parameter is displayed when the Request parameter Type is set to 1.

Score.description: The probability score for making a phone call, with a value range of (0,1).

Threshold.description:
Recommended threshold.

> For reference only. In actual applications, select an appropriate Score threshold based on testing results.

Score.description: The probability score for wearing earphones, with a value range of (0,1).

Threshold.description:
Suggested threshold.  

> This is provided for reference only. In actual applications, select an appropriate Score threshold based on testing results.

PersonNumber.description: The total number of detected persons. A value of 0 indicates that no examinee was detected.

Threshold.description:
Recommended threshold, with a value range of (0,1). When ChatScore exceeds the threshold, the system determines that the input screen capture contains a chat tool.  
This parameter is displayed when the request parameter Type is set to 0.

post.description: This topic describes the syntax and sample of the PedestrianDetectAttribute structured human body property. The gateway injects Alibaba RPC Action=PedestrianDetectAttribute and Version automatically before forwarding the paid request upstream.

ImageURL.description: The image URL. We recommend using an OSS link in the China (Shanghai) region. If your file is stored locally or in an OSS bucket outside the China (Shanghai) region, see [File URL Processing](~~155645~~).

Data.description: The content of the returned result data.

Name.description: AgeOver60 (older than 60 years); Age18-60 (between 18 and 60 years); AgeLess18 (younger than 18 years).
Yes (has a backpack); No (does not have a backpack).
Yes (has a handbag); No (does not have a handbag).
Yes (wearing a hat); No (not wearing a hat).
Black, gray, blue, green, white, purple, red, brown, yellow, pink, uncertain.
Trousers (long pants); Shorts (short pants); Skirt&Dress (skirts or dresses).
Black, gray, blue, green, white, purple, red, brown, yellow, pink, uncertain.

Boxes.description: Coordinates of the detected pedestrian bounding boxes.

BottomRightX.description: X-coordinate value of the bottom-right corner of the bounding box.

BottomRightY.description: Y-coordinate value of the bottom-right corner of the bounding box.

TopLeftX.description: The x-coordinate value of the top-left corner of the bounding box.

TopLeftY.description: The y-coordinate value of the top-left corner of the bounding box.

Height.description: Height of the original image, in pixels.

PersonNumber.description: The number of detected pedestrians. The value is a positive integer.

Width.description: Width of the original image, in pixels.

post.description: This topic describes the syntax and examples of the RecognizeAction operation for action behavior detection. The gateway injects Alibaba RPC Action=RecognizeAction and Version automatically before forwarding the paid request upstream.

Type.description:
Input content type, including video and image types.

- 0: Indicates a video URL or a Base64-encoded video string.

- 1: Indicates an image URL or a Base64-encoded image string.

- Others: Invalid input.

items.description: Image list

URL.description: Image URL required when Type is 1. **You must provide exactly four images. These images should be uniformly sampled from a two-second video, and must have identical resolution and number of channels.** We recommend using an OSS link in the China (Shanghai) region. If your file is stored locally or in an OSS bucket outside the China (Shanghai) region, see [File URL Processing](~~155645~~).

imageData.description: Base64-encoded image string. When provided together with URL, URL takes precedence. For instructions on Base64 encoding files, see [File Base64 Processing](~~468191~~).

VideoData.description: Base64-encoded video string. When provided together with VideoURL, VideoURL takes precedence. For instructions on Base64 encoding files, see [File Base64 Processing](~~468191~~).

VideoUrl.description: Video URL required when Type is 0. We recommend using an OSS link in the China (Shanghai) region. If your file is stored locally or in an OSS bucket outside the China (Shanghai) region, see [File URL Processing](~~155645~~).

Data.description: The content of the returned result data.

Boxes.description: List of bounding box coordinates for the specific action.

items.description: Coordinate bounding box information for specific actions.
Detected behavior classes.

Confidence level for each behavior class. The value ranges from 0 to 1. A higher value indicates a more accurate detection of the action. The system provides threshold standards for different actions based on the algorithm. You can also adjust these thresholds according to your actual requirements.

- Raising hand: 0.5 (If below 0.5, it is not considered raising hand; otherwise, it is considered raising hand.)

- Eating or drinking: 0.5 (If below 0.5, it is not considered eating or drinking; otherwise, it is considered eating or drinking.)

- Smoking: 0.5 (If below 0.5, it is not considered smoking; otherwise, it is considered smoking.)

- Making a phone call: 0.5 (If below 0.5, it is not considered making a phone call; otherwise, it is considered making a phone call.)

- Using a phone: 0.5 (If below 0.5, it is not considered using a phone; otherwise, it is considered using a phone.)

- Sleeping on a desk: 0.5 (If below 0.5, it is not considered sleeping on a desk; otherwise, it is considered sleeping on a desk.)

- Falling down: 0.5 (If below 0.5, it is not considered falling down; otherwise, it is considered falling down.)

- Washing hands: 0.5 (If below 0.5, it is not considered washing hands; otherwise, it is considered washing hands.)

- Taking a photo: 0.5 (If below 0.5, it is not considered taking a photo; otherwise, it is considered taking a photo.)

Timestamp.description: UNIX timestamp indicating when the current behavior occurred in the video or image.

post.description: This topic describes the syntax and sample code of the RecognizeExpression operation. The gateway injects Alibaba RPC Action=RecognizeExpression and Version automatically before forwarding the paid request upstream.

ImageURL.description: The URL of the image. We recommend using an OSS link in the China (Shanghai) Region. For files stored locally or via an OSS link outside the China (Shanghai) Region, see [File URL Processing](~~155645~~).

Data.description: The content of the returned result data.

Elements.description: The detection results of each sub-element.

Expression.description:
The expression class. Valid values:

- neutral: neutral
- happiness: happy
- surprise: surprised
- sadness: sad
- anger: angry
- disgust: disgusted
- fear: fearful
- pouty: pouting
- grimace: grimacing

FaceProbability.description: The probability of a face, with a value range from 0 to 1.

Height.description: The height of the face area.

Left.description: The x-coordinate of the top-left corner of the face area, with the origin at the top-left corner of the image.

Top.description: The y-coordinate of the top-left corner of the face area, with the origin at the top-left corner of the image.

Width.description: The width of the face area.

post.description: Detects five facial attributes: gender, age, expression, glasses, and hat, and returns a 1024-dimensional deep learning feature of the face. The gateway injects Alibaba RPC Action=RecognizeFace and Version automatically before forwarding the paid request upstream.

Age.description: This parameter is compatible with the older version of the API and controls whether the facial age detection result should be returned. Valid values are true or false. Default value: false.

Beauty.description: Compatible with the legacy API to control whether to return facial attractiveness results. Valid values are true or false. Default value: false.

Expression.description: Compatible with the legacy API to control whether to return facial expression results. Valid values are true or false. Default value: false.

Gender.description: Compatible with the legacy API to control whether to return gender detection results. Valid values are true or false. Default value: false.

Glass.description: Compatible with the legacy API to control whether to return eyeglasses detection results. Valid values are true or false. Default value: false.

Hat.description: Compatible with the legacy API to control whether to return hat detection results. Valid values are true or false. Default value: false.

ImageURL.description:
The image URL. We recommend using an OSS link in the China (Shanghai) region. For local files or OSS links outside the China (Shanghai) region, see [File URL Processing](~~155645~~).

> - The recommended approach for the new version is to input only the image. When only ImageURL is provided, all API data will be returned. The new version supports returning data for up to 10 faces.

- To maintain compatibility with the legacy API, when any supported Boolean input parameter or MaxFaceNumber is provided to control the returned data, only the data corresponding to Boolean input parameters set to true will be returned. This invocation method does not return data outside the scope supported by the Boolean input parameters (Landmarks, LandmarkCount, PoseList, Pupils).

Mask.description: Compatible with the legacy API to control whether to return face mask detection results. Valid values are true or false. Default value: false.

MaxFaceNumber.description: Compatible with the legacy API to specify the maximum number of faces to return from the image. Valid values range from 1 to 10. Default value: 1. To return results for multiple faces, set this parameter correctly. Results are sorted in descending order by default based on the FaceProbabilityList parameter.

Quality.description: Compatible with the legacy API to control whether to return facial quality results. Valid values are true or false. Default value: false.

Data.description: The Content of the returned Result Data.

items.description: The age of each detected face in the image, with values ranging from 0 to 100. If multiple faces are detected, the ages are returned in order.
Beauty score. A higher value indicates higher attractiveness, with a range of (0–100].

DenseFeatureLength.description: The dimension of the feature vector returned by facial recognition, currently fixed at 1024.

items.description:
The facial recognition features returned by the system. If multiple faces are detected, the results are returned in order.

> This field has been deprecated due to a version upgrade and no longer contains meaningful data.

The facial expression result from facial recognition. It includes:

- 0: Neutral

- 1: Smile

Returns the probability of face detection, with values ranging from 0 to 1. If multiple faces are detected, the probabilities are returned in order. For example, if two faces are detected, the result is `[face_prob1, face_prob2]`.

Returns face bounding rectangles as `[left, top, width, height]`. If multiple faces are detected, their rectangles are appended sequentially. For example, two faces would result in `[left1, top1, width1, height1, left2, top2, width2, height2]`.  
left-top: The top-left corner of the bounding box, using the top-left corner of the Image as the coordinate origin (x, y). This point is x pixels from the left border and y pixels from the top border of the Image.  
width-height: The width and height of the Target bounding box.  
The area of the bounding box is width × height, and the bottom-right coordinate is (left + width, top + height).

The detected gender of each face. If multiple faces are detected, the results are returned in order.

- 0: Female

- 1: Male

Indicates whether the face is wearing glasses.

- 0: Not wearing glasses

- 1: Wearing regular glasses

- 2: Wearing sunglasses

Indicates whether the face is wearing a hat.

- 0: No hat

- 1: Wearing a hat

LandmarkCount.description: The number of facial feature points detected. Currently fixed at 105 points, distributed as follows: 24 points for eyebrows, 32 points for eyes, 6 points for nose, 34 points for mouth, and 9 points for the outer contour.

items.description: The localization results of facial feature points. Each detected face returns one group of feature point coordinates in the format `(x0, y0, x1, y1, ...)`. If multiple faces are detected, the results are returned in order as floating-point numbers.

Whether each detected face is wearing a mask.

- 0: Not wearing a mask  
- 1: Wearing a mask  
- 2: Wearing a mask incorrectly

The pose of each detected face, formatted as `[yaw, pitch, roll]`. If multiple faces are detected, the results are returned in order.

- Yaw represents the left-right rotation angle, ranging from -90 to 90 degrees.

- Pitch represents the up-down rotation angle, ranging from -90 to 90 degrees.

- Roll represents the in-plane rotation angle, ranging from -180 to 180 degrees.

The centroid coordinates and radius of the left and right pupils. For each face, six floating-point numbers are provided in the order: `[left_iris_cenpt.x, left_iris_cenpt.y, left_iris_radius, right_iris_cenpt.x, right_iris_cenpt.y, right_iris_radis]`.

Qualities.description: Face quality information. A higher score indicates better conditions for detection.

items.description: The Impact score of face blur on detection. A higher score is more favorable for detection, with a value range of (0,100]. If multiple faces are present, the scores are listed in sequence. During detection, it is recommended to set a threshold greater than or equal to 85 (you can decide whether to set a threshold and its value based on your actual application scenario). A score above 85 indicates a lower probability that the image is blurred, while a score below 85 indicates a higher probability of blur.

The score indicating whether the target is a face and its Impact on detection. A higher score is more favorable for detection, with a value range of (0,100]. If multiple faces are present, the scores are listed in sequence. During detection, it is recommended to set a threshold greater than or equal to 85 (you can decide whether to set a threshold and its value based on your actual application scenario). A score above 85 indicates a higher probability that the image contains a face, while a score below 85 indicates a lower probability that the image does not contain a face.

The Impact score of upper-face occlusions, such as glasses, on detection. A higher score is more favorable for detection, with a value range of (0,100]. If multiple faces are present, the scores are listed in sequence. During detection, it is recommended to set a threshold greater than or equal to 85 (you can decide whether to set a threshold and its value based on your actual application scenario). A score above 85 indicates a lower probability that the person is wearing glasses, while a score below 85 indicates a higher probability of wearing glasses.

The Impact score of illumination on facial recognition. A higher score indicates better conditions for recognition. The value range is (0, 100]. If multiple faces are present, the scores correspond to each face in sequence. During recognition, it is recommended to set a threshold greater than or equal to 85 (you can decide whether to set a threshold and its value based on your actual application scenario). A score above 85 indicates a higher probability of good image illumination, while a score below 85 indicates a lower probability of good image illumination.

The Impact score of lower-face occlusions, such as masks, on facial recognition. A higher score indicates better conditions for recognition. The value range is (0, 100]. If multiple faces are present, the scores correspond to each face in sequence. During recognition, it is recommended to set a threshold greater than or equal to 85 (you can decide whether to set a threshold and its value based on your actual application scenario). A score above 85 indicates a lower probability of wearing a mask, while a score below 85 indicates a higher probability of wearing a mask.

The Impact score of image noise on detection. A higher score is more favorable for detection, with a value range of (0,100]. If multiple faces are present, the scores are listed in sequence. During detection, it is recommended to set a threshold greater than or equal to 85 (you can decide whether to set a threshold and its value based on your actual application scenario). A score above 85 indicates a lower probability that the image contains noise, while a score below 85 indicates a higher probability of noise.

The Impact score of pose on facial recognition. A higher score indicates better conditions for recognition. The value range is (0, 100]. If multiple faces are present, the scores correspond to each face in sequence. During recognition, it is recommended to set a threshold greater than or equal to 85 (you can decide whether to set a threshold and its value based on your actual application scenario). A score above 85 indicates a higher probability of a frontal face pose, while a score below 85 indicates a lower probability of a frontal face pose.

Overall quality score. A higher value indicates better suitability for detection, with a range of (0, 100]. If multiple faces are present, scores are returned in corresponding order. During detection, we recommend setting a threshold of at least 85 (you can determine whether to apply a threshold and its specific value based on your actual application scenario). A score above 85 indicates higher overall image quality, while a score below 85 indicates lower overall image quality.

post.description: This topic describes the syntax and examples of RecognizePublicFace. The gateway injects Alibaba RPC Action=RecognizePublicFace and Version automatically before forwarding the paid request upstream.

ImageData.description:
The Base64-encoded string of the image. When both this parameter and the URL method are provided, the URL method takes precedence.  
For instructions on Base64 encoding files, see [Base64 Encoding for Files](~~468191~~).

ImageURL.description:
The URL of the image to be detected. Each element in the JSON array represents an image detection task (image Table). A maximum of 10 elements is supported, meaning up to 10 images can be processed simultaneously. We recommend using OSS links from the China (Shanghai) region. For local files or OSS links from other regions, see [File URL Processing](~~155645~~).

> For this API, the system automatically converts the OSS URL to an internal network address before downloading the file. Therefore, invoking this API does not incur OSS traffic billing.

Data.description: The content of the returned result data.

ImageURL.description: The image URL from the corresponding request.

Results.description: The return result. When the invocation succeeds, the result contains one or more elements.

Label.description:
The categorization of the detection result. Valid values include:

- normal: The image is normal and does not contain sensitive faces.

- sface: The image contains a sensitive face.

Rate.description: The confidence level of the detection result, with a value range of `[0.00,100.00]`. A higher value indicates a greater probability that the image belongs to this class.

SubResults.description:
A list of detected face information.  
> When multiple faces are present in the image, only the face with the highest confidence level is returned by default.

Name.description: Name of the recognized similar person.

H.description: The height of the face area.

W.description: The width of the face area.

X.description: The distance from the top-left corner of the face area to the x-axis, with the top-left corner of the image as the coordinate origin.

Y.description: The distance from the top-left corner of the face area to the y-axis, with the top-left corner of the image as the coordinate origin.

Suggestion.description:
The operation recommended for you to execute. Valid values:

- pass: The image is normal and requires no further action.

- review: The detection result is uncertain and requires manual review.

- block: The image violates policies, and you are advised to take further action (such as directly deleting it or applying restrictions).

TaskId.description: The ID of the detection job.

post.description: This topic describes the syntax and examples of Intelligent RetouchSkin. The gateway injects Alibaba RPC Action=RetouchSkin and Version automatically before forwarding the paid request upstream.

ImageURL.description:
Image URL. We recommend using an OSS link in the China (Shanghai) region. For local files or OSS links in other regions, see [File URL Processing](~~155645~~).  

Image format requirements:  

- Image formats: JPEG, JPG, PNG.  
- File size: No more than 6 MB.  
- Resolution: Greater than 128×128 pixels and less than or equal to 5000×5000 pixels, with the longest edge no greater than 5000 pixels.  
- Image content: Photos containing 1 to 10 human portraits with clearly visible skin areas.  
- The URL must not contain Chinese characters.  

> Image content: If the image resolution exceeds the maximum limit, scale down the image first. For details, see [Image Scaling](~~44688~~).

RetouchDegree.description: Skin smoothing intensity. Valid values: [0, 1.5]. Default value: 1.0. A higher value results in less visible skin texture.

WhiteningDegree.description: Whitening intensity. Valid values: [0, 1.5]. Default value: 1.0. A higher value results in whiter skin.

Data.description: Returned data content.

ImageURL.description:
URL of the retouched image.  
> This URL is temporary and has a validity period of 30 minutes. After expiration, the URL will no longer be accessible. To retain the file for an extended or permanent duration, you must access the URL and download the file within 30 minutes, then store it in your own OSS bucket or another storage space.
